meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve no coding experience (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no coding experience. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Coding Without Experience
content.seo_description: Overcome no-code barriers with this expert project management checklist. Master Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first professional AI application.
content.answer_block: To build complex software without traditional coding skills, treat your project like a product roadmap. Use Antigravity to architect the logic and Claude Code to execute the implementation. By breaking features into atomic tasks and leveraging AI agents, you bridge the technical gap between your initial idea and a production-ready application.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank terminal, the cursor blinking with the weight of a thousand 'what-ifs.' The gap between your innovative vision and the reality of complex syntax feels like an unscalable mountain. Many students freeze here, convinced that without a CS degree, their project is destined to remain a prototype in a folder that no one will ever see.

But the rules of software development have shifted. The era of the 'AI Builder' means your ability to architect logic and prompt execution is now more valuable than knowing every semicolon in a language. We are going to deconstruct your project into manageable, high-impact milestones, turning that overwhelming mountain into a series of climbable, rewarding steps.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP
steps.description: Strip your idea down to the single core feature that provides value. Avoid feature creep by documenting the primary user flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Break down my app idea into a 3-tier feature list: MVP, Phase 2, and Future Roadmap. Focus on the most critical user path.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README.md outlining the MVP scope and user requirements.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Use AI to define your data structures before writing a single line of code. This prevents architectural debt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for my app. Output the structure in JSON format, defining all entities, relationships, and data types.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a schema.prisma file based on the provided data structure.'

steps.title: Implement Modular Logic
steps.description: Build your application in isolated modules. This makes debugging significantly easier when something breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular folder structure for a Next.js application. Explain the purpose of each directory and how to maintain separation of concerns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a project folder structure and install the necessary dependencies for a modular architecture.'

steps.title: Automated Code Generation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to translate your logic requirements into functional components, keeping files small and focused.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, reusable React component for the main user dashboard based on the schema defined in Step 2.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the dashboard component with basic error handling and prop validation.'

steps.title: Continuous Integration Testing
steps.description: Implement automated checks to ensure your AI-generated code meets quality standards and doesn't introduce regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the dashboard component using Jest, covering edge cases and potential null inputs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run the test suite and fix any identified issues automatically.'

pro_tips: Always define your 'Definition of Done' for every task before asking the AI to write the code.
Use Claude Code to iterate on existing files rather than generating massive files from scratch to maintain context window efficiency.
Document every AI prompt in a 'Prompt Library' file within your repo to reproduce successful patterns later.
When the AI hits a wall, ask it to 'explain its current reasoning' before asking for a code fix to catch logic errors early.
Prioritize readability over brevity; AI-generated code is easier to debug when it is explicitly commented and modular.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is functional, focus on deploying to Vercel and setting up a continuous deployment pipeline to share your progress with the community.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build professional-grade software. Stop waiting for the 'right time' and start shipping your vision today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
